claim: Eighth MCC measurement (Session 9, Path A attempt). n=20 balanced panel at scale=0.05, t_end=5.0 s (10x the short-window baseline), ensemble per_rule_with_pool_confirm with min_pool_dev=0.10. Result: MCC=0.000 (TP=3, FP=3, TN=7, FN=7). The longer window does NOT improve discrimination - pool deviations grow for both TPs AND FPs. Specifically, transporter-KO 0034 shows max_pool_dev=13.3 at t_end=5.0 s (up from ~1.0 at t_end=0.5 s) because its upstream metabolite accumulates unboundedly in the simulator. **Path-A falsified at scale=0.05**: extending bio-time does not close the simulator-biology gap because the simulator lacks the biological consumers / pathway redundancy that mask a KO's effect in a real cell.

reference_panel_side_result.note: pgi + ptsG caught; ftsZ correctly negative; 0305 (Quasi) missed (no catalytic rules). Similar to v5-ref because per-rule detection is t_end-independent for uniquely-catalysed rules.

false_positives.interpretation: Transport KO makes an upstream metabolite accumulate unboundedly in the simulator at t_end=5.0 s - in a real cell, either the metabolite would be consumed by alternate pathways or diffusion equilibrium would cap its rise. Simulator models neither.
Same 0.167 as at t_end=0.5 s; time-independent.
Same 0.167 as at t_end=0.5 s; time-independent.

wall_stats.note: 10x bio-time gives 6.7x wall-time at scale=0.05 (sub-linear: setup amortised across longer run).

caveats: Below every prior balanced-panel MCC. Reporting as measured - no post-hoc explanations to inflate.

Path A (longer bio-time) is falsified at scale=0.05. It does not help because the simulator lacks biological pathway redundancy - extending time just lets the FP pool deviations grow alongside the TP ones. This was the explicit hypothesis queued from Session 8.
The full 458-gene sweep at t_end=5.0 s (estimated 3 h wall in Session 8 NEXT_SESSION) was NOT run - the n=20 result makes that commitment unjustified.

Honest path to MCC > 0.59 now requires a simulator biology upgrade: pathway-redundancy modelling so that Breuer-Nonessential catalytic genes' KOs are absorbed by alternate enzymes. That is a multi-session simulator commitment, not a detector-side fix.

Reference-panel n=4 result is still optimistic at 0.577 - matches v5-ref because PerRule is the component doing the work. When scaled to a balanced n=20 with a realistic ratio of catalytic vs non-catalytic genes, the signal dilutes.

The n=20 sample is approximately 50/50 Essential/Nonessential; of the 10 Essential, only ~3 (pgi, tpiA, ACPPAT-catalyser) have catalysis rules that uniquely identify them. The other 7 Essentials are non-catalytic (ribosomal, tRNA, replication) and architecturally invisible to this detector family - same as v5. The cap on achievable MCC given the current detector + simulator is bounded by this ratio.
